home.heroSubtitle: Step into a smoky 80s detective agency, question suspects with SQL queries, and crack the case one statement at a time.
home.welcomeMessage: The city is buzzing. New evidence just landed on your desk.
home.featureQueryDrivenDesc: Every clue is hidden in a database. Use SQL to interrogate the data and expose the culprit.
home.featureNoSetupDesc: Built-in SQL workspace powered by SQL.js. Just open a case and start digging.
home.featureEarnXPDesc: Solve cases to earn XP and level up your detective badge as you take on tougher investigations.
home.howItWorksP1: Each case drops you into a story-driven investigation. You read the brief, study the database schema, and run SQL queries in a built-in editor to surface clues. As you connect tables, eliminate suspects, and validate alibis, you practice real SQL patterns: filtering, joins, aggregates, and subqueries. No setup or installs. Just open a case file and start interrogating the data.
home.howItWorksP2: Your XP and solved-case count track your progress, so you can start as a rookie and work up to the advanced cases at your own pace. Beginner cases teach fundamentals, while advanced cases push you with layered joins and trickier conditions.
home.howItWorksP3: Ready to see it in action?
home.audienceDevelopers: Developers wanting a hands-on, story-first way to practice SQL fundamentals and joins.
home.audienceAnalysts: Data analysts preparing for interviews who need realistic querying scenarios.
home.audienceStudents: Students who learn faster through narrative and immediate feedback instead of dry textbooks.
home.audienceInstructors: Instructors looking for engaging SQL exercises without any environment setup for learners.
home.faqSubtitle: Quick answers before you start your first case.
home.faqAccountQ: Do I need an account to play?
home.faqAccountA: You can open and solve cases without creating an account. Sign in to track XP, progress, and solved cases across devices.
home.faqAccessA: Three starter cases are free to play, no account needed. The remaining cases unlock with a one-time Detective License, a single lifetime purchase rather than a subscription. XP tracks your progress as you solve cases.
home.faqSqlQ: What SQL do I need to know?
home.faqSqlA: Beginners can start with simple SELECTs. Intermediate and advanced cases introduce joins, grouping, filters, and subqueries as you progress.
home.faqInterviewA: Yes. Cases mimic realistic data puzzles you might see in data and engineering interviews. It's great for practicing under a narrative without rote question banks.
home.faqHintsQ: What if I get stuck on a case?
home.faqHintsA: Every case gives you a schema view and a list of objectives to work from, plus a notes panel to track your thinking. If you're still stuck, the community Discord and the help page are good places to ask.

metadata.title: Privacy Policy
metadata.description: SQLNoir privacy policy - how we collect, use, and protect your data when you play our interactive SQL detective game.

body.title: Privacy Policy
body.collectionIntro: When you use SQLNoir, we may collect the following information:
body.collectionAccount: Account data: If you sign in with Google OAuth (via Supabase), we store your email address and a user ID.
body.collectionAnalytics: Analytics data: We use PostHog and Vercel Analytics to collect anonymized page views and feature usage data.
body.collectionCookies: Cookies: Authentication session cookies (Supabase) and analytics cookies (PostHog).
body.useItem1: To provide and improve SQLNoir's features and content.
body.useItem2: To track your progress, XP, and solved cases (if signed in).
body.useItem3: To understand how users interact with the site so we can make it better.
body.cookiesAuth: Authentication cookies (Supabase) - to keep you signed in across sessions.
body.cookiesAnalytics: Analytics cookies (PostHog) - to collect anonymized usage data.
body.cookiesDisable: You can disable cookies in your browser settings. Disabling authentication cookies will require you to sign in again on each visit.
body.thirdPartyStripe: Stripe - processes payments for paid Detective Licenses.
body.thirdPartyNoSell: We do not sell your data to any third parties.
body.retentionBody: Account data is retained for as long as your account is active. Analytics data is retained in anonymized form and automatically expires based on PostHog and Vercel's retention policies.
body.rightsIntro: Under GDPR and other applicable privacy regulations, you have the right to:
body.rightsItem1: Access the personal data we hold about you.
body.rightsItem4: Opt out of analytics tracking by enabling the Do Not Track setting in your browser. PostHog respects the DNT signal.
body.rightsExercise: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at the email below.
body.contactBody: If you have any questions about this privacy policy or want to request data deletion, contact us at <email>support@sqlnoir.com</email>.

metadata.title: Terms of Service
metadata.description: SQLNoir terms of service - rules and conditions for using our interactive SQL learning platform and detective cases.

body.title: Terms of Service
body.overviewBody: SQLNoir is an interactive SQL learning platform where you solve detective cases using SQL queries. By using SQLNoir, you agree to these terms. SQLNoir is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind.
body.accountsIntro: You can use SQLNoir without creating an account. Signing in with Google OAuth allows you to track progress across devices.
body.accountsFreeTier: Free access: Three starter cases are free to play - no account or payment required. The remaining cases are unlocked with a one-time Detective License.
body.accountsLicense: Detective License: Three starter cases are free. Unlocking the remaining cases requires a one-time Detective License - a single lifetime payment, not a subscription. The license never expires and includes future paid cases at no extra cost.
body.paymentsBody: Three starter cases are free to play. Unlocking the remaining cases requires a one-time Detective License, a single lifetime payment with no recurring or monthly charges. Payments are processed securely by <stripe>Stripe</stripe>. If you experience any issues, please contact us and we'll do our best to help.
body.refundBody: We offer a 14-day, no-questions-asked refund, and we'll help any unhappy customer even after that window. To request a refund, email <email>support@sqlnoir.com</email>.
body.ugcBody: SQL queries you write in SQLNoir are executed locally in your browser using SQL.js. We do not store, collect, or share your queries.
body.acceptableUseItem1: Use automated tools to scrape or crawl SQLNoir content.
body.acceptableUseItem2: Attempt to disrupt or abuse the service.
body.acceptableUseItem3: Redistribute or resell case content without permission.
body.ipBody: All cases, stories, characters, and code on SQLNoir are copyright of SQLNoir and its creator. You may not reproduce or redistribute this content without written permission.
body.terminationBody: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms.
body.changesBody: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of SQLNoir after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.
body.contactBody: Questions about these terms? Contact us at <email>support@sqlnoir.com</email>.

metadata.title: FAQ
metadata.description: Answers to common questions about SQLNoir's lifetime Detective License, refunds, country pricing, free cases, offline play, and support.

body.title: Frequently Asked Questions
body.intro: Common questions about buying and playing SQLNoir. Can't find what you need? Email support@sqlnoir.com.

q1.question: Is this a subscription or a one-time purchase?
q1.answer: It's a one-time purchase, not a subscription. You buy a lifetime Detective License once and it never expires. The license also includes future paid cases we release, at no extra cost. There are no recurring charges.

q2.question: I paid but can't access the cases. What do I do?
q2.answer: This usually happens when the purchase was made anonymously, before signing in. Sign in with any account, reopen a locked case, and click Get License to claim your purchase. If you're still locked out, email <email>support@sqlnoir.com</email> and we'll sort it out.

q3.question: Can I get a refund?
q3.answer: Yes. We offer a 14-day, no-questions-asked refund, and we'll help any unhappy customer even after that window. Just email <email>support@sqlnoir.com</email>.

q4.question: Why is the price different in my country?
q4.answer: We use fair purchasing-power pricing. The price is automatically adjusted to the local purchasing power of your country, so it may differ from what others see. This is intentional, not a bug.

q5.question: Which cases are free?
q5.answer: Three starter cases are free to play. The remaining cases require a Detective License.

q6.question: Can I play offline?
q6.answer: You can work on a case in your browser without a connection, but checking your answers requires an internet connection.

q7.question: Do you offer team, classroom, or education licenses?
q7.answer: Yes, with discounts. Email <email>support@sqlnoir.com</email> for a quote and we'll set you up.

q8.question: How do I get help or report a bug?
q8.answer: Email <email>support@sqlnoir.com</email> or join our <discord>Discord</discord> community. We're happy to help.
